events.label: assistant
events.text:
Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: **needs_more_info**, high risk.

Key reasoning: the trap-to-tee connection itself looks fine, but the fixture's vent can't be verified — the wall is already closed/finished, no rough-in photo exists, and no vent data was supplied. Two things would settle it: (1) rough-in photos or as-built documentation showing the vent tie-in, trap arm length/slope, and sanitary-tee orientation, or (2) a camera/borescope inspection (or reopening the wall) if no such record exists. Until then it can't be passed, since an unverified or missing vent risks trap-seal loss and sewer gas entry, and closing DWV work before inspection is itself a process violation.
